title: rhodiola rosea subsp. neomexicana
intro: Rhodiola rosea subsp. neomexicana, also known as New Mexico rhodiola, is a subspecies of the Rhodiola rosea plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for stress, anxiety, and fatigue. The health benefits of Rhodiola rosea subsp. neomexicana include improved mood, reduced stress levels, and enhanced cognitive function, as well as its potential to improve physical performance and endurance. Rhodiola rosea subsp. neomexicana can be prepared into various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
